_comment: Blog posts in publishing order. Adding a .md to /blog/ without an entry here keeps it as a draft (unreachable by URL slug). Order is by published_at desc at render time; raw order here doesn't matter beyond keeping diffs sane.

posts.title: Wire your Renogy gear to WattPost instead of using a BT-2 dongle
posts.summary: For metal-van installs, long cable runs, or anywhere your BT-2 dongle keeps dropping out. Step-by-step setup with USB-RS485 + Cat5e: cable picks, pinout, daisy-chain vs Hub vs multi-adapter topologies, and the by-id path that survives reboots.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: Read your Victron gear over cable when Bluetooth isn't reliable
posts.summary: WattPost v0.1.23 adds a wired alternative to BLE Instant Readout. Metal-van builds and dense-RF environments where Bluetooth keeps dropping now have a path that just works. Step-by-step setup with Victron's VE.Direct cable.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: What shipped in WattPost v0.1.22
posts.summary: The smart-plug output adapter the solar-pause rule was missing. Shelly Gen2 and Tasmota over local HTTP, no broker, no Home Assistant, no cloud. Plus a correction to last week's v0.1.21 notes.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: Pause your AC charger with a smart plug when the sun is doing the work
posts.summary: Step-by-step setup for the solar-pause rule with a Shelly Plug S Gen2 sitting between the wall and your AC charger. Off-grid friendly, no broker, no Home Assistant required.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: What shipped in WattPost v0.1.21
posts.summary: Reset-to-defaults for Docker users, the new solar-aware charger pause rule engine (Pro tier), a dashboard snapshot-lock fix that stops the hero and flow tiles disagreeing with each other, plus a strategic coverage roadmap that finally writes down which drivers come next.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: Connect WattPost to Home Assistant without the Mosquitto add-on
posts.summary: If you're on Home Assistant Container, Core, or you already have a standalone Mosquitto broker running for the rest of your house, the add-on shortcut doesn't apply. Stand up your own broker in Docker, point both WattPost and HA at it, and get the same MQTT auto-discovery experience.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: What shipped in WattPost v0.1.20
posts.summary: Three customer-facing changes: a wizard hint for the Bluetooth ownership conflict we lost a day to, deeper BLE diagnostics for the Realtek+BlueZ silent-scan bug, and editable polling cadence + history retention in Settings.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: WattPost vs Victron VRM in Home Assistant
posts.summary: Honest head-to-head for anyone on a Victron stack with VRM today, considering whether to switch. Pure-Victron with a Cerbo: stay on VRM. Mixed-stack or no Cerbo: WattPost is cheaper and faster.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: Add WattPost to Home Assistant's Energy dashboard
posts.summary: HA ships a built-in Energy dashboard. Three clicks and the right sensors and you get daily / monthly / yearly solar production charts, source-vs-use breakdown, and long-term aggregation, all from your WattPost MQTT feed.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: Low-SoC alerts via Home Assistant
posts.summary: Two automations, ten minutes of work, never wake up to a dead battery again. Includes a recovered notification and a quieter belt-and-braces version that uses WattPost's own alert engine for redundancy.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: Home Assistant Lovelace dashboards for off-grid solar
posts.summary: Copy-paste YAML for three useful Lovelace views: a daily-driver overview, a today-vs-yesterday energy summary, and a deep-dive battery dashboard. Stock Home Assistant cards only, no HACS required.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: How to add WattPost to Home Assistant in five minutes
posts.summary: WattPost ships MQTT auto-discovery out of the box. Point Home Assistant at your appliance, flip one toggle, and every battery, charger, and shunt shows up as a discovered device. No YAML, no helper scripts.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: How to set up WattPost on a Raspberry Pi from scratch
posts.summary: Blank SD card to a live solar dashboard in one sitting. Download, flash, boot, pair your first Renogy or Victron device, and (optionally) link to the cloud for remote access.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: How a laptop on the same network made our Renogy BT-2 dongle invisible
posts.summary: We lost a day to a BT-2 that refused to advertise. The fix was three words on a different host. Here's the debugging story and the wizard hint we shipped so the next person catches it in minutes.
posts.author: Ritual North

posts.title: Welcome to the WattPost blog
posts.summary: Building a local-first off-grid solar monitor in public. Tutorials, release notes, customer stories, and the occasional debugging war story.
posts.author: Ritual North
